identity.short_name: Count F3 3x3 matrices with fixed nilpotent Jordan chain

subdomain(s): minimal polynomial and Jordan canonical form
topic_path: Jordan form with constrained chain counting

problem_spec.summary: Count 3x3 matrices over a finite field satisfying a matrix polynomial identity and trace/rank constraints that force a unique Jordan form, with extra linear constraints fixing both a kernel generator and a specific generalized eigenvector mapping to it.
givens: Chain constraints: Av=0 and Aw=v for v=(1,1,1)^T, w=(1,0,0)^T.
ask: Count the number of matrices A satisfying all constraints.

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: sets the finite-field arithmetic and counts of subspaces

parameter(s).name: annihilating_polynomial
parameter(s).purpose: restricts possible eigenvalues and Jordan block sizes

parameter(s).name: trace_value
parameter(s).purpose: forces eigenvalue multiplicities once eigenvalues are restricted

parameter(s).name: rank_value
parameter(s).purpose: forces a nontrivial Jordan block rather than diagonalizability

parameter(s).name: kernel_vector_v
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the 1D kernel line when dim ker(A)=1

parameter(s).name: generalized_vector_w
parameter(s).purpose: fixes a specific generalized eigenvector in the forced Jordan chain

invariant(s).name: eigenvalue_set_from_polynomial
invariant(s).statement: From A^2(A-I)=0, the minimal polynomial divides x^2(x-1), so eigenvalues lie in {0,1} and 0-block sizes are \leq2.

invariant(s).name: multiplicities_from_trace
invariant(s).statement: With eigenvalues in {0,1} and n=3, tr(A)=1 forces algebraic multiplicities (n_1,n_0)=(1,2).

invariant(s).name: non_diagonalizable_forced_by_rank
invariant(s).statement: With (n_1,n_0)=(1,2), diagonalizability would give rank 1; rank(A)=2 forces a size-2 Jordan block at eigenvalue 0.

invariant(s).name: chain_is_fixed
invariant(s).statement: If dim ker(A)=1 and Av=0, then ker(A)=⟨v⟩; adding Aw=v fixes the length-2 Jordan chain (w\tov\to0) inside the generalized 0-eigenspace.

essential_assumption(s): All arithmetic and ranks are over 𝔽_3.
The vectors v and w are linearly independent (w ∉ ⟨v⟩), ensuring the chain constraint is consistent with rank(A)=2.
Similarity is via GL_3(𝔽_3), preserving the polynomial identity, rank, and trace.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The constraints A^3=A^2, tr(A)=1, and rank(A)=2 force a unique Jordan form up to similarity: diag(1,J_2(0)).
The constraints Av=0 and Aw=v fix the nilpotent Jordan chain; remaining freedom is exactly the choice of the 1-eigenline complementary to span(v,w), yielding a finite, uniquely determined count.

technique(s).name: polynomial_to_minimal_polynomial_bound
technique(s).purpose: restrict eigenvalues and allowable Jordan block sizes

technique(s).name: rank_forces_nondiagonalizable_at_zero
technique(s).purpose: force a size-2 Jordan block (rule out diagonalizable projection)

technique(s).name: enforce_fixed_jordan_chain
inputs: Av=0 and Aw=v with dim ker(A)=1
technique(s).purpose: fix a specific generalized eigenvector chain within the nilpotent part

technique(s).name: orbit_stabilizer_with_flag
inputs: canonical Jordan form and fixed chain
technique(s).purpose: count matrices via stabilizers/centralizers respecting the fixed vectors

theorem(s).name: Minimal polynomial divides an annihilating polynomial
Jordan Canonical Form (split characteristic polynomial)
theorem(s).role: classify the unique Jordan type consistent with constraints

theorem(s).name: Orbit-Stabilizer Theorem
theorem(s).role: count flagged conjugacy realizations via group actions

critical_trick(s): Use A^3=A^2 to pin eigenvalues to {0,1} and bound nilpotent block sizes.
Use tr(A)=1 to force characteristic polynomial (x-1)x^2.
Use rank(A)=2 to force a nontrivial Jordan block at eigenvalue 0.
Interpret Av=0 and Aw=v as fixing an ordered Jordan chain; count remaining freedom by stabilizers or by counting complement eigenlines.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: enumerate all 3^9 matrices over 𝔽_3 for brute-force verification

library(s).name: numpy
library(s).reason: fast matrix multiplication mod 3 and vector checks

task(s).name: brute_force_verify_count
task(s).reason: enumerate all A\inM_3(𝔽_3), filter by constraints, and confirm the count

task(s).name: structural_sanity_checks
task(s).reason: confirm rank and polynomial identity for found matrices; optionally verify similarity invariants (e.g., characteristic polynomial)
